title: New Evangelion series announced
wikitext:
[]: New Evangelion series announced             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Mon Feb 23, 2026 5:08 am tweet-body — twitter-user (@username) date document.querySelectorAll('blockquote.twitter-tweet > a').forEach((t) => t.href = t.href.replace('x.com', 'twitter.com')) Yoko Taro (Nier series) is writing. Uhhh...odd lol. Tsurumaki directing, hes not leaving Eva jail.

[]: Re: New Evangelion series announced             Quote Post by DTGee » Mon Feb 23, 2026 5:25 am Very interesting that this doesn't seem to have anything to do with any previous version of Eva. Not Rebuild, not Anima, not Raising Project... It's its own new unique thing. Also, super glad that Tsurumaki has finally been given the chance to lead his own Eva project free from Anno.

[]: Re: New Evangelion series announced             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Feb 23, 2026 5:26 am We're here forever, it seems.

[]: Re: New Evangelion series announced             Quote Post by JB2448 » Mon Feb 23, 2026 5:33 am Eva is a story that repeats. https://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2 ... ion-movies NieR has a lot of the same underlying pathos. This honestly isn't that far fetched, but I never would have thought it would happen. Day one. https://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2 ... es/.234476

[]: Re: New Evangelion series announced             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Mon Feb 23, 2026 5:38 am I don't want to be an asshole, but Yoko Taro really feels a bit like a "meme choice". Like a very obvious "what do young people like now" choice. He's also admitted Nier is basically Eva, so there's a risk I guess of having something similar again. Not if he is self aware and fights that, of course. Tsurumaki co-directing feels to me like they're still not trusting the new generation of khara to take over. Which after 20 years of khara existing is like...bro. I hope the female director is given control (it's the same directing duo as the -46 hours short). Not saying this won't be good, but I'm not immediately feeling this. Most excited about CloverWorks getting to animate Eva.

[]: Re: New Evangelion series announced             Quote Post by DTGee » Mon Feb 23, 2026 5:48 am kuribo-04 wrote: Tsurumaki co-directing feels to me like they're still not trusting the new generation of khara to take over. Which after 20 years of khara existing is like...bro.

[]: Re: New Evangelion series announced             Quote Post by Joseki » Mon Feb 23, 2026 6:00 am Yoko Taro feel both a great choice and a "fan cast", but I really liked Automata and you could evidently see the NGE tones in all his works. Definitely not Evangelion inspired moment from Automata Mild Automata spoiler out of context  SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: New Evangelion series announced             Quote Post by Weird_ocean » Mon Feb 23, 2026 6:45 am First thing that comes to mind is that the Asuka Short is the preface for the new series, that will be about Asuka. Timing seems anything but random. Tsurumaki is an Asuka fan, Yoko Taro is a EVA fan... One thing I'm glad about is that Maki or Anno won't write it, because Rebuild and Gquaaax were kind of awfully written, although they certainly will influence this story big time. Well, the Gundamafication of EVA has started as the Scrolls of the dead Anno predicted. I don't know how I feel about this, But I knew Shin will not be the end of it. Too much money on the table. Fuck, we'll see I guess, but I'm not hyped about it, because too many times in my life hype got the better of me and got my hopes up. I'll judge it when I'll see the preview at least, and decide if I want to watch it or not. I already was burned once with Rebuilds.

[]: Re: New Evangelion series announced             Quote Post by Joseki » Mon Feb 23, 2026 6:57 am Weird_ocean wrote: One thing I'm glad about is that Maki or Anno won't write it, because Rebuild and Gquaaax were kind of awfully written, although they certainly will influence this story big time.

[]: Re: New Evangelion series announced             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Mon Feb 23, 2026 7:03 am GQuuuuuuX was good IMO, which is more than I could say for NieR Automata. Seriously, I just do not have high expectations for this with Taro writing.

[]: Re: New Evangelion series announced             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Mon Feb 23, 2026 7:13 am Mr. Tines wrote: We're here forever, it seems.

[]: Re: New Evangelion series announced             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Mon Feb 23, 2026 7:21 am I think I don't have much to add here. Will obviously watch when it comes out, but right now it's a bit meh news. At least Yoko Taro won't get dumb nerd hate, since he already has a cult following. Anyone less famous would get destroyed online if they don't live up to Anno's masterpiece(s). Not talking criticism, you know what I mean, toxic nerd behaviour.

[]: Re: New Evangelion series announced             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Feb 23, 2026 7:25 am From /a/, about the announcement Trailer was very short and didn't reveal much, but here's a description >it shows the old building where kaworu was playing his piano and it’s all a big ruin and on the top of said ruin is a violin, and it’s all covered in a big amount of grass and leaves

[]: Re: New Evangelion series announced             Quote Post by Weird_ocean » Mon Feb 23, 2026 7:27 am One thing I think we can say for certain, if it's Tsurumaki + Taro + Cloverworks at the wheel, expect a soft porn level of fanservice. Like cosmic level of buttshots in this bitch.

[]: Re: New Evangelion series announced             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Mon Feb 23, 2026 7:31 am Weird_ocean wrote: One thing I think we can say for certain, if it's Tsurumaki + Taro + Cloverworks at the wheel, expect a soft porn level of fanservice. Like cosmic level of buttshots in this bitch.

[]: Re: New Evangelion series announced             Quote Post by Joseki » Mon Feb 23, 2026 8:03 am Mr. Tines wrote: From /a/, about the announcement Trailer was very short and didn't reveal much, but here's a description >it shows the old building where kaworu was playing his piano and it’s all a big ruin and on the top of said ruin is a violin, and it’s all covered in a big amount of grass and leaves

[]: Re: New Evangelion series announced             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Mon Feb 23, 2026 8:19 am tweet-body — twitter-user (@username) date document.querySelectorAll('blockquote.twitter-tweet > a').forEach((t) => t.href = t.href.replace('x.com', 'twitter.com')) Trailer.

[]: Re: New Evangelion series announced             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Mon Feb 23, 2026 8:26 am If nothing else it looks and sounds good. Hard for me to tell if they're intentionally trying to evoke Rebuild there or its just because Taro likes moss-covered ruins imagery generally.

[]: Re: New Evangelion series announced             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Mon Feb 23, 2026 8:31 am DTGee wrote: Not Rebuild, not Anima, not Raising Project... It's its own new unique thing.

[]: Re: New Evangelion series announced             Quote Post by Joseki » Mon Feb 23, 2026 8:35 am The instruments appear to be a mix of the piano from Rebuild and the string quartet from Death. Eva 01 is redesigned, seems more "feral". Music is pretty much Okabe, not much to say really.
